title: rauvolfia semperflorens var. semperflorens
intro: Rauvolfia semperflorens var. semperflorens is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, also known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a climber with medicinal properties attributed to its roots, which contain a variety of alkaloids, including ajmaline and serpentine, that have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety, treating hypertension, and alleviating symptoms of epilepsy, due to its ability to interact with neurotransmitters in the brain. Herbal preparations of Rauvolfia semperflorens var. semperflorens typically involve drying and powdering the roots, which can be used in capsules, tablets, or teas, and is often prescribed in Ayurvedic and traditional Chinese medicine.
